headline: Structural Clarity Through the Rule of Three
shortStatement: We don't guess at market position — we reveal the structural reality of your competitive landscape.
description: Every market naturally organizes into generalists and specialists. The Rule of Three framework provides a rigorous, evidence-based lens to understand where a company sits in this structure and what strategic options are actually available. This isn't about aspirational positioning — it's about understanding the structural forces that determine success.
supportingPoints: Proprietary Rule of Three Checklist covering all 5 player types
Strategic element mapping: core strategy, key tactics, critical insights per player type
Market development phase analysis (Start Up → Growth → Mature)
Specialist vs generalist characteristic comparison across 6 dimensions

headline: Top-Tier Rigor, Boutique Delivery
shortStatement: The analytical depth of a Big Three firm, delivered with the speed and directness of a focused partner.
description: Our 35-question assessment framework across 9 categories delivers the same structured, evidence-based analysis you'd get from Bain, BCG, or McKinsey. But because we're a focused boutique, you get direct partner involvement in every engagement, faster turnaround, and a co-creative model that builds ownership from day one.
supportingPoints: Board-ready deliverables with full data backing
Direct founder involvement — no junior consultants running your project

headline: Strategy That People Execute
shortStatement: A strategy nobody understands is just a document. We build strategies that organizations adopt.
description: With deep expertise in change management, cultural transformation, and group dynamics, we don't just define strategy — we ensure it lands. The strategy house framework makes complex strategic direction visually clear and organizationally actionable. Our co-creative process builds buy-in during development, not after.
supportingPoints: Strategy house visualization connecting vision → business cases → initiatives → enablers → foundation
Co-creative methodology with regular check-ins and shared ownership
Cultural transformation experience across financial services and manufacturing
Performance-linked pricing (85/15 model) that proves confidence in adoption

headline: Built for the Dutch Mid-Market
shortStatement: We understand the dynamics of Dutch companies scaling beyond their comfort zone.
description: The Dutch mid-market has specific strategic challenges: family-owned businesses going international, manufacturing companies navigating consolidation, services firms competing against both local specialists and global generalists. We understand these dynamics because that's our focus — not a side practice within a larger firm.
supportingPoints: Amsterdam-based with Dutch, English, and Spanish capability
Client portfolio spanning automotive, manufacturing, textiles, financial services, and business services
Understanding of Dutch business culture — direct, pragmatic, relationship-driven
